The key information here pertains to understanding what happens when the teeth come into occlusion. Occlusion involves specific parts coming into contact - specifically, the cusps of the molars interact such that the maxillary palatal cusps interdigitate with the mandibular facial cusps. Therefore, we focus on "cusp-to-cusp" interaction rather than edge-to-edge, marginal, or surface-to-surface interactions.
